summary: In autosomal dominant inheritance, a heterozygous affected parent has a 50% chance of transmitting the mutant allele to each offspring, showing vertical transmission.
Autosomal dominant traits skip generations, showing horizontal transmission.
Both parents must carry the mutation for autosomal dominant expression.
Autosomal recessive disorders require two copies of the mutant allele (homozygous state) for phenotypic expression; carrier parents are heterozygous.
Autosomal recessive disorders manifest in heterozygous individuals.
Carrier parents of autosomal recessive disorders have a 50% risk of affected children.
Consanguinity (marriage between close relatives) increases the risk of autosomal recessive disorders by increasing the chance of homozygosity.
Consanguinity only increases the risk of autosomal dominant disorders.
Consanguinity has no effect on the frequency of genetic disorders.
X-linked recessive disorders predominantly affect males because they have only one X chromosome, so a single mutant allele causes disease.
X-linked recessive disorders affect males and females equally.
Males can be carriers of X-linked recessive disorders.
An affected father with an X-linked recessive disorder cannot transmit it to his sons (he gives them his Y chromosome) but all his daughters will be carriers.
An affected father transmits X-linked recessive conditions to all his sons.
Daughters of affected fathers are never carriers.
Anticipation is the phenomenon where a genetic disease becomes more severe or has earlier onset in successive generations, seen in trinucleotide repeat disorders.
Anticipation means a disease becomes milder in successive generations.
Anticipation is exclusive to autosomal recessive disorders.
Variable expressivity means the same mutation produces different degrees of clinical severity in different individuals, while penetrance refers to the proportion who show any phenotype at all.
Variable expressivity and penetrance mean the same thing.
Penetrance describes the severity of the disease in affected individuals.
